sourceMovie.title: The Master
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Master for the same reason it broke through in 2012: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Paul Thomas Anderson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Lucky One
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Master clicked for you, The Lucky One (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Jacob's Ladder
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Master clicked for you, Jacob's Ladder (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Master?
faq.answer: Try The Lucky One, Jacob's Ladder, Cherry—each shares DNA with The Master while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Master?
faq.answer: The Master (2012) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Master worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
